title: Pandanus calathiphorus
taxonomy: Pandanus calathiphorus is classified under the family Pandanaceae. The genus Pandanus is native to tropical regions of the world and includes over 750 species. As a member of the Pandanus genus, Pandanus calathiphorus is part of a diverse group of plants known for their long, slender stems and feathery leaves. The family Pandanaceae is a group of monocotyledonous plants, characterized by their single seed leaf and often woody stems. Pandanus calathiphorus is further classified within the Pandanaceae family as a distinct species with its unique characteristics.

The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. intro: Pandanus calathiphorus is a species of plant in the Pandanaceae family, commonly known as a type of screwpine or pandan. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Pandanus calathiphorus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
